If you ever saw, there's a famous episode of Tom Snyder's show. Remember the Tomorrow Show with Tom Snyder? And so he always had like only one guest. That was one of the best things about the show. It was a real in-depth interview with somebody who actually could be interviewed rather than five minutes of some bubble head on, you know, the Leno show. And so he's got one guest and he's got an hour, you know. And so they stuck him one night. They brought in this author that was fake. And for an hour, I mean, this guy would only give a monosyllabic answer. So no matter what the question was, the answer was yes. No. And you literally could watch Snyder getting more and more desperate and more and more pissed off, right? Because his questions are getting longer and longer and longer and longer and longer. You know, he's very much aware of time. And I mean, they didn't tell him until the next day. It was great. So I thought about it. But I mean, the point is, either with each other or with you, there's no sensitivity to what any of you thought. It's not the point. And really, very much immune to it. Most people are not immune. So they are affected by what all sorts of other people think about them. And now it affects how they manage employees. It affects how they interact with customers and clients. If they deal with the media, it affects how they deal with the media. It affects all sorts of things. And essentially, it puts them in a smaller place of opportunity with less room to move than if they don't care, if they're immune to it, right? And again, at a lesser income level, you can get that in this smaller place where you keep bumping your hip up against this thing that makes you restate or edit or, you know. But at this income level, you need more room. So a lot of the problems are in this second category. And we're going to talk a little bit more about those before we abandon this topic. The third, and this was evident in a lot of questionnaires. It was said many different ways. But the summation of it is a fear of a change in the dynamic of relationships with others or in how you have to live or even change in your belief system caused by the bigger income. And I'd say it was probably said in half of the questionnaires one way or another. And so there's concern, really now, of what are other people going to think of me and say about me because I am doing so well. There's concern that everybody's going to try and take advantage of me and line up and want some. So there's a long list of these, but they can all be summarized as that. And what I would say about it is that they're all true. So every one of those fears is 1,000% valid. They're all going to happen. The dynamic of relationships you have with other people changes. How could it not? How could it not? So if you make $2 million a year and somebody makes $200,000 a year, how can the dynamic of the relationship not change? Everything from who picks up the tab to how the person feels about you behind your back. It has to change. Somebody on a questionnaire said, let's see if I can find it. Yeah. What's most surprised you about moving up an income so far? The people around you aren't necessarily happy for you or proud of you. Greatest cause of anxiety becoming a target. Well, both of those things are true. The people around you aren't necessarily happy for you. For a variety of reasons. They think that by the luck of the draw, you should have would have been them. They kind of think you're an idiot anyway. And so it's grossly unfair.
